title: Integrated rotor devices for autonomous analytical centrifugation, integrated cell devices for autonomous analytical centrifugation, and methods of assembly and operation of same
abstract: A rotor system comprises a rotor constructed and arranged to rotate about an axis of rotation. A source of electromagnetic radiation is positioned at a first position of the rotor, the source of electromagnetic radiation configured to emit electromagnetic radiation at one or more wavelengths. The rotor system further includes a sample region. A detector is positioned at a second position of the rotor, the detector constructed and arranged to receive electromagnetic radiation that traverses at least a portion of the sample region.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1. A rotor system, comprising: a rotor constructed and arranged to rotate about an axis of rotation; a source of electromagnetic radiation at a first position of the rotor, the source of electromagnetic radiation configured to emit electromagnetic radiation at one or more wavelengths; a sample region; and a detector at a second position of the rotor, the detector constructed and arranged to receive electromagnetic radiation that traverses at least a portion of the sample region. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the rotor comprises a central opening. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the central opening is aligned with the axis of rotation. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the rotor is constructed and arranged to mate with a spindle. 5: The system of claim 4, wherein the spindle is aligned with the axis of rotation. 6. The system of claim 4, wherein the spindle comprises a spindle of a centrifuge. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the rotor is oriented symmetrically about the axis of rotation. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the rotor comprises one or more sub-units configured to be stacked in a vertical direction of extension along the axis of rotation. 9. The system of claim 8, wherein neighboring ones of the one or more stacked sub-units are coupled to each other at a threaded interface. 10. The system of claim 8, wherein one or more of the one or more stacked sub-units are coupled with a bolt-through configuration. 11.
